svg.text: Audio Processing using Transformer — Complete Workflow
Raw waveform → Feature extraction → Transformer encoder/decoder → Task output
Raw waveform — 1-D amplitude signal over time
Mono / stereo WAV, MP3, FLAC · Sample rates: 8 kHz (telephone), 16 kHz (speech), 44.1 kHz (music)
float32 amplitude array, shape [T] where T = duration × sample_rate e.g. 5 s × 16000 Hz = 80 000 samples
STAGE 3 — FEATURE EXTRACTION (converting audio to 2-D feature maps)
Log-Mel Spectrogram ← most common Transformer input
Final shape: [n_mels=80, T_frames] — transposed to [T_frames, 80] for the Transformer
STAGE 4 — PATCH / FRAME EMBEDDING + POSITIONAL ENCODING
• Analogous to token embedding in NLP
• Whisper uses 2 × Conv1d layers instead of linear
• Rotary (RoPE) — used in modern LLM-audio models
Output: [batch, T_frames, d_model] — ready for Transformer
Q = K = V = input X (self-attention over frames)
H heads run in parallel, concat → Linear
Captures long-range temporal dependencies in audio
e.g. H=8 heads, d_k=64, total d_model=512
Each frame attends to ALL frames
② Fine-tune with task labels (≥10 hr)
④ Freeze encoder, train head only
⑤ Evaluate on dev set → early stop
2017 Transformer · 2020 wav2vec2 · 2021 HuBERT / AST · 2022 Whisper / AudioLM · 2023 MusicGen / Seamless · 2024 Qwen-Audio / Gemini-Audio
